title: Toads in Trouble: A Sad Story from a Reservoir
summary: In Wrexham, a group called the Toad Patrol helps over 1,000 toads cross a busy road every year to reach a reservoir where they breed. This year, the water company drained the reservoir to make safety improvements over Easter weekend. The volunteers were shocked because the toads were still breeding and would have left in a few weeks. When they checked, the reservoir was silent with no toads seen, making them fear the toads died. Toads are important for nature, and their numbers have been dropping in the UK. The volunteers are upset and wish they had known earlier to try to save the toads. They help by gathering and moving toads, frogs, and newts during breeding season from February to May. This story shows how human actions can harm animals and why we need to work together to protect them.

keywords.explanation: amphibians that need water to breed
a big man-made lake for storing water
when water is removed from a place
all living things in an area that depend on each other

questions.question: What do the Toad Patrol volunteers help toads do?
When did the volunteers check the reservoir and find it silent?
What do the volunteers fear happened to the toads?
During which months do volunteers help toads?
Why are reservoirs important for toads?

background_read: Toads are amphibians that live on land but need water to lay eggs and breed. In the UK, toad numbers have been going down because there are fewer ponds in gardens. Volunteers in Wrexham form groups called Toad Patrols to help toads cross roads safely during breeding season from February to May. They do this because roads can be dangerous for toads. Reservoirs are big lakes made by humans to store water, and they can be important homes for toads. When a reservoir is drained, it means the water is taken out, which can harm animals living there. This story teaches us about helping wildlife and how our actions affect nature.
Article_Structure: WHO: Volunteers from Wrexham Toad Patrols and the Hafren Dyfrdwy water company
WHAT: The reservoir was drained, possibly killing over 1,000 breeding toads
WHERE: Nant-y-Ffrith reservoir in Wrexham, UK
WHY: The water company drained it for safety improvements, but it happened during breeding season, harming the toads

perspectives.description: They are upset and fear the toads died because the reservoir was drained during breeding season without warning.
They say they drained the reservoir for essential safety improvements, but did not mention the toads.
